|RALST0N PLEADS
| | FOR v. W.P. co.

pages.raw_text:
City Engineer Ralston was the
chief witness for the Washington
Water Power company yesterday at
Occur d'Alene in its effort to show
that the municipality Of Spokane
will suffer grievous loss If it Is
made to cease flooding the lands of
the farmers abutting Lake Occur
d'Alene. Mr.. Ralston told of the
severe loss that will be entailed by
the city If the government compels
the power trust to remove it's bear
trap dam at Post Falls. Ralston
figured that II the dam is removed
II will mean loss to the city in
pumping capacity of 40 per cent in
the season of low water In the sum-
Iner time, when water is needed
the most. He said that 1t would
mean decrease of 20,000,000 gal
Ions daily pumping capacity In the
contemplated pumpage ability of
54000,000 gallons in "a hours.
